summary: Comprehensive testing of Passwordless Auth features (forgot password, magic login, passwordless signup, set password via settings). Backend: 17/17 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements present and functional. Found 1 bug: token-based magic links won't work due to incorrect JWT purpose field.

critical.issue: Token-based password reset will fail. _generate_verification_code() creates tokens with purpose='email_verify' but reset-password expects purpose='password_reset'. Code-based flow works correctly.
critical.fix: Create separate token generation functions for each purpose OR modify _generate_verification_code to accept purpose parameter

critical.issue: Token-based magic login will fail. _generate_verification_code() creates tokens with purpose='email_verify' but verify-magic-login expects purpose='magic_login'. Code-based flow works correctly.
critical.fix: Same as above - need purpose-specific token generation

passed_tests: Backend: Passwordless signup with just {email, name} returns needsVerification=true
Backend: Passwordless user has empty password_hash (not null due to DB constraint)
Backend: Passwordless user has has_password=false after verification
Backend: Magic login sends code and returns success
Backend: Magic login verify with correct code returns JWT token
Backend: Magic login with wrong code returns 400 error
Backend: Magic login for non-existent user returns generic success (security)
Backend: Forgot password sends reset code
Backend: Forgot password for non-existent user returns generic success (security)
Backend: Reset password with code+newPassword sets new password and returns token
Backend: Can login with new password after reset
Backend: Reset password with wrong code returns 400 error
Backend: Reset password with short password returns 400 error
Backend: Set password via settings updates password and returns has_password=true
Backend: Can login with password after setting it via settings
Backend: Set password via settings with short password returns 400 error
Backend: Verified user login with password returns JWT token with has_password=true
Frontend: Login page shows 'Forgot password?' link
Frontend: Login page shows 'Sign in with email link' button
Frontend: Register page shows 'Sign up with email only' button
Frontend: Clicking 'Sign up with email only' shows passwordless signup form (name + email only, no password)
Frontend: Clicking 'Forgot password?' shows forgot password flow (enter email -> enter code -> set new password)
Frontend: Clicking 'Sign in with email link' shows magic login flow (enter email -> enter code)
Frontend: Settings modal shows 'Change Password' section for users with password
Frontend: Settings modal shows 'Set a Password' section for passwordless users
Frontend: All data-testid attributes present for testing
action_items: FIX (HIGH): Modify _generate_verification_code() to accept a 'purpose' parameter and use it in the JWT token. Currently it always uses 'email_verify' but reset-password expects 'password_reset' and magic-login expects 'magic_login'. This breaks the magic link (token-based) flows.
Alternative fix: Create separate functions for each purpose: _generate_email_verify_code(), _generate_password_reset_code(), _generate_magic_login_code()
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: _generate_verification_code() at line 262-270 always uses purpose='email_verify' in JWT token, but reset-password (line 633) expects 'password_reset' and verify-magic-login (line 718) expects 'magic_login'. This breaks token-based flows.
Code-based flows (email + 6-digit code) work correctly for all features
Security: password_hash never exposed in API responses (stripped by strip_password function)
Security: Non-existent user requests return generic success messages (don't reveal if user exists)
has_password flag correctly derived from checking if password_hash is non-empty string
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_passwordless_auth.py - New comprehensive test file for passwordless auth features

test_credentials.note: Created/verified for testing purposes - email_verified=true, has_password=true

seed_data_creation: Created/verified test_verified_user@testdomain.com with email_verified=true and password for testing verified user login flow
context_for_next_testing_agent: Passwordless auth features tested. Key findings: 1) Code-based flows (email + 6-digit code) work correctly for all features, 2) Token-based flows (magic links) will fail due to incorrect JWT purpose field - needs fix, 3) Passwordless users have empty password_hash (not null) and has_password=false, 4) Settings modal shows 'Set a Password' for passwordless users and 'Change Password' for users with password. Test user test_verified_user@testdomain.com exists with password TestVerified123! for future testing.

features_verified.details: POST /api/auth/signup with just {email, name} creates user with empty password_hash and returns needsVerification
After verification, passwordless user has has_password=false in response
POST /api/auth/magic-login sends code, POST /api/auth/verify-magic-login with email+code returns token
Token has wrong purpose field - will fail validation
POST /api/auth/forgot-password sends code, POST /api/auth/reset-password with email+code+newPassword works
Token has wrong purpose field - will fail validation
PUT /api/user/settings with {new_password} updates password and returns has_password=true
All buttons, forms, and flows present with correct data-testid attributes

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The _generate_verification_code() function at line 262-270 in server.py always generates JWT tokens with purpose='email_verify'. However, the reset-password endpoint (line 633) expects purpose='password_reset' and the verify-magic-login endpoint (line 718) expects purpose='magic_login'. This mismatch causes token-based flows to fail with 'Invalid reset token' or 'Invalid login token' errors.
reproduction_steps: 1. Request forgot password: POST /api/auth/forgot-password with valid email
2. Get the token from the email link (or construct from DB)
3. Try to reset password with token: POST /api/auth/reset-password with {token, newPassword}
4. Observe error: 'Invalid reset token' because token has purpose='email_verify' not 'password_reset'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Modify _generate_verification_code() to accept a 'purpose' parameter: def _generate_verification_code(user_id: str, purpose: str = 'email_verify'). Then update callers: forgot_password should pass purpose='password_reset', magic_login should pass purpose='magic_login'.
